title: Button Groups
intro: Button groups are the commitment points of every screen — modal footers, drawer footers, toolbars, page-level commit bars. One gap (8px), one primary per group, one ordering rule. This entry consolidates the four spacing values (8/10/12/20px) that had drifted across the codebase into a single standard.

items.rule: All adjacent buttons sit 8px apart (`--space-2`) — footers, toolbars, action bars, no exceptions.
items.why: The codebase had drifted to 8/10/12/20px gaps across cousin screens. One value makes clusters read as a unit and removes per-screen judgment calls.

items.rule: Exactly one `theme1` button per group — every other action takes the default appearance.
items.why: Two filled-primary buttons split the commitment; the user can no longer tell which action the screen is for.

items.rule: Order is escalation of commitment: secondary first, primary last (rightmost).
items.why: Reading left to right, the user passes the escape hatch (Cancel) before reaching the commitment (Save). Primary-first reverses muscle memory from every other Gravitate screen.

items.rule: Destructive actions are isolated on the far left with `justifyContent='space-between'`; Cancel + primary stay grouped right.
items.why: Physical distance prevents misclicks on irreversible actions. A Delete button nestled beside Save is one slip away from data loss.

items.rule: Theme semantics are fixed: `theme1` = primary commit (save, submit, create), `success` = positive completion only (approve, publish, mark complete), `error` = destructive (delete, remove, discard), default = secondary (cancel, close, back).
items.why: A green Submit button reads as "already done." When every screen honors the same mapping, color alone communicates consequence.

items.rule: Commit clusters right-align (`justifyContent='flex-end'`) in modal, drawer, and panel footers.
items.why: Terminal actions live at the end of the reading path. Centered button clusters read as marketing pages, not workflow.

items.rule: Page-level commit bars use the shared `ActionFooter` / `StepFooter` primitives — never a hand-rolled flex strip.
items.why: Four cousin implementations (ContractManagement, quick-entry FooterBar, ManageOffers wizard, QuoteBook publisher) each invented their own padding, height, and ordering. The primitives encode the rules above so they cannot drift.

blocks.title: Theme semantics
blocks.caption: The four button meanings: theme1 (primary commit), success (positive completion only), error (destructive), and default (secondary). Each prop is a boolean — there is no type or theme string prop.

blocks.title: Button ordering
blocks.caption: Standard order (Cancel left, primary right) and the three-button pattern (destructive isolated far-left via space-between, Cancel + primary grouped right at 8px).

blocks.title: Standard footer
blocks.caption: The canonical modal/drawer/panel footer: horizontal flex, flex-end, 8px gap, secondary before primary.

blocks.title: Spacing tokens
blocks.intro: Three values cover every spacing decision inside a button group.

rows.use: Gap between all adjacent buttons — footers, toolbars, action bars
Gap between a button and adjacent text or a status badge
Icon-to-text inside a button — Ant Design applies this automatically; never add manual margins

blocks.title: Canonical skeletons
blocks.code:
import { Horizontal, GraviButton } from '@gravitate-js/excalibrr'

// Standard footer — modals, drawers, panels
<Horizontal gap={8} justifyContent='flex-end'>
  <GraviButton buttonText='Cancel' onClick={onClose} />
  <GraviButton buttonText='Save Changes' theme1 onClick={() => form.submit()} />
</Horizontal>

// Three-button — destructive isolated left
<Horizontal justifyContent='space-between'>
  <GraviButton buttonText='Delete Draft' error onClick={onDelete} />
  <Horizontal gap={8}>
    <GraviButton buttonText='Cancel' onClick={onClose} />
    <GraviButton buttonText='Save' theme1 onClick={onSave} />
  </Horizontal>
</Horizontal>
blocks.note: Gap and justification are Horizontal props, never style objects. Form submission goes through onClick={() => form.submit()} — htmlType='submit' is not used with GraviButton.

blocks.title: GraviButton essentials
blocks.intro: The props that matter for grouping. Theme props are booleans; set exactly one — when several are truthy the component resolves them by fixed internal precedence, not your JSX order.

rows.notes: The button label. Pass buttonText, never children — Excalibrr 4.x drops JSX children silently (empty button, no error); 5.x falls back to children only when buttonText is absent.
Primary commit action. The one filled-brand button per group.
Positive completion only — approve, publish, mark complete. Never for submit/save.
Destructive action — delete, remove, discard. This is the prop name; there is no danger boolean in the Excalibrr theme set.
Typed in ThemeVariants but not wired in the installed 5.x lib — it renders as a default button. Prefer a confirm dialog over a yellow button.

rows.default: solid when a theme prop is set; outlined otherwise
rows.notes: Use 'outlined' for the lower-emphasis variant — the value is 'outlined', not 'outline' ('outline' silently coerces to filled).
Leading icon. Ant Design handles the 4px icon-to-text gap; add no manual margin.

blocks.title: Page footer primitives
blocks.intro: Page-level commit bars — the strip at the bottom of a page's main content area — come from @components/shared/PageFooter. The caller composes the button cluster, so different flows (Save / Save Draft / Publish) work without prop sprawl.

rows.name: ActionFooter
rows.when: Single-screen commit. Optional icon + title left, optional notice/status slot, button cluster right.
rows.code: <ActionFooter title='Quote Publisher' notice={<StatusBadge tone='warning' variant='quiet' label='3 unsaved adjustments' />} actions={<>…</>} />

rows.name: ActionFooter emphasis='title'
rows.when: The footer title labels the entire page (Contract Management pattern). Adds a 2px brand rule under the title block — use sparingly or emphasis becomes noise.

rows.name: ActionFooter compact
rows.when: Dense grid pages where the standard 48px bar steals canvas. 40px height, tighter padding.

rows.name: StepFooter
rows.when: Multi-step wizards. Previous + dot indicator + tabular-numeric 'Step N of M' + Next/Finish. The last step swaps Next for Finish automatically.

blocks.title: ActionFooter — single-screen commit
blocks.caption: ActionFooter with icon + title left, quiet status notice, and a three-button cluster right — Reset and Save Adjustments default, Publish Prices as the lone theme1.

blocks.title: StepFooter — wizard
blocks.caption: StepFooter mid-wizard: Previous left, advancing dot indicator with 'Step 2 of 4' center, Next as the primary right.

items.do: Use theme1 for the primary action with an 8px gap: <GraviButton buttonText='Cancel' /> then <GraviButton buttonText='Create Contract' theme1 />.
items.dont: Use success for a submit/create button, or pad the cluster to 12px.
items.why: success means approve or complete — a green Create button claims the work is already done, and off-grid gaps make sibling screens look unrelated.

items.do: Isolate the destructive button far-left with space-between; keep Cancel + primary grouped right at 8px.
items.dont: Center the cluster, put primary before Cancel, or mix Delete into the right-hand group.
items.why: Reversed order breaks the escalation pattern, and a Delete button adjacent to Save is one misclick from data loss.

items.do: Self-close and pass the label: <GraviButton buttonText='Save' theme1 />.
items.dont: Pass the label as children: <GraviButton theme1>Save</GraviButton>.
items.why: buttonText renders on every Excalibrr version; 4.x drops JSX children silently, producing an empty button with no error or warning.

blocks.title: Do's & don'ts, rendered
blocks.caption: Side-by-side: correct theme1 primary at 8px vs. green success primary at 12px; isolated destructive vs. centered reversed cluster at 20px.

items.title: GraviButton renders buttonText, not children
items.detail: Always self-close and pass buttonText. Excalibrr 4.x drops JSX children silently — the button renders empty with no error; 5.x falls back to children only when buttonText is absent. This has bitten multiple sessions; it is the number-one GraviButton bug.

items.title: Themes are boolean props, and destructive is `error`
items.detail: Write <GraviButton theme1 />, <GraviButton success />, <GraviButton error />. There is no type='primary', no theme='success' string, and no danger boolean in the Excalibrr theme set — antd's danger prop bypasses Gravitate theming.

items.title: appearance='outlined', not 'outline'
items.detail: The lower-emphasis appearance value is 'outlined'. 'outline' is silently coerced to 'filled' — you get a filled button instead of the outlined one you asked for.

items.title: No htmlType='submit'
items.detail: GraviButton does not participate in native form submission. Wire the primary to onClick={() => form.submit()} so Ant Design's Form validation pipeline runs.

items.title: Layout props, not style objects
items.detail: Gap and justification belong on the layout primitive: <Horizontal gap={8} justifyContent='flex-end'>. style={{ gap: '8px' }} on Horizontal is the lint-flagged anti-pattern.

blocks.title: Where this applies
blocks.body:
Every multi-button cluster: modal footers, drawer footers, inline panel footers, toolbars above grids, and page-level commit bars. For modals and drawers, build the footer by hand with the canonical skeleton. For page-level bars, reach for `ActionFooter` or `StepFooter` and compose the buttons into the `actions` slot — the primitive owns padding, height, and alignment so the only decisions left are which buttons and which one gets `theme1`.

A single button next to text (an empty-state CTA, a card action) follows the same semantics: `theme1` if it is the screen's commitment, default otherwise, 8px from the adjacent text.
